preamble: The shift from the voiceless velar lateral affricate [kʟ] to the voiceless alveolar lateral affricate [tɬ] is a process of fronting. This change involves moving the place of articulation from the soft palate forward to the alveolar ridge.

examples.note: Fronting of velar laterals in certain modern speakers or dialects.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
